mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so a sustained decline in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in any 3-year rolling window, or if the sign flips positive during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which raises recession risk and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the average forward return is nonnegative across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening employment and stronger final demand, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to beat the unconditional SPX hit rate over any 3-year window, or if either series alone fully explains the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO relative to prior months signals industrial contraction and weaker cyclical growth, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its value 2 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if months with falling INDPRO do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than months with stable or rising INDPRO, or if the conditional sign remains positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness indicates housing demand stabilization and lower recession probability, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds are not followed by higher 60-day SPX returns than non-rebound periods, or if the relationship is absent in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is elevated and FEDFUNDS is below CPI inflation, real policy rates are negative and liquidity conditions are accommodative, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS below inflation do not outperform other periods on 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect reverses during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS together with a rising UNRATE captures simultaneous payroll loss and unemployment deterioration, a recessionary labor shock that should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-loss and unemployment-rise condition does not materially lower 20-day SPX returns relative to either signal alone, or if the hit rate is not below 50% across triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime, weak GDPC1 growth combined with falling RSAFS indicates shrinking real activity and sales, so S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should be negative until the contraction ends.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns during contraction periods, or if positive returns dominate despite continued declines in both series.
